name: Monstera
notes: Allow top 2 inches of soil to dry out between waterings.

name: Pothos
notes: Very forgiving. Drooping leaves signal it needs water.

name: Snake Plant
notes: Drought-tolerant. Overwatering is the main risk.

name: Peace Lily
notes: Will visibly droop when thirsty — a natural reminder.

name: Fiddle Leaf Fig
notes: Hates being moved. Water when top inch is dry.

name: ZZ Plant
notes: Extremely drought-tolerant. Thrives on neglect.

name: Rubber Plant
notes: Wipe leaves occasionally to keep them glossy.

name: Bird of Paradise
notes: Loves lots of light. Water thoroughly then let drain.

name: Spider Plant
notes: Very adaptable. Brown tips often mean tap water sensitivity — try filtered water.

name: Aloe Vera
notes: Let soil dry completely between waterings. Excellent drainage essential.

name: Calathea
notes: Sensitive to minerals. Use filtered or rainwater. Loves humidity.

name: Chinese Evergreen
notes: One of the most tolerant houseplants. Avoid cold drafts.

name: Dracaena
notes: Sensitive to fluoride. Let water sit overnight before using.

name: Boston Fern
notes: Loves humidity. Mist regularly or place on a pebble tray with water.

name: Philodendron
notes: Similar to pothos. Allow top inch of soil to dry between waterings.

name: String of Pearls
notes: Unique succulent. Water sparingly — root rot is the main issue.

name: Jade Plant
notes: Succulent — drought-tolerant. Water deeply then let dry completely.

name: Parlour Palm
notes: One of the best low-light palms. Keep soil evenly moist.

name: African Violet
notes: Water from below to avoid spotting leaves. Keep soil moist but not soggy.

name: Cast Iron Plant
notes: Lives up to its name — nearly indestructible. Avoid direct sun.

name: Orchid
notes: Water by soaking roots for 15 min weekly. Allow to dry between waterings.

name: Cactus (Barrel)
notes: Water very sparingly. Almost no water needed in winter.

name: Heartleaf Philodendron
notes: Fast-growing and forgiving. Trail or let climb.

name: Umbrella Plant
notes: Allow soil to dry slightly between waterings. Good drainage is key.

name: Oxalis
notes: Leaves fold at night — totally normal. Will go dormant occasionally.

name: Croton
notes: Needs lots of light for vivid colour. Drops leaves if moved or stressed.

name: Air Plant
notes: Soak in water for 20–30 min weekly. Shake off excess and let dry upside down.

name: Ponytail Palm
notes: Stores water in its bulbous trunk. Very drought-tolerant.

name: Hoya
notes: Thrives on neglect. Let soil dry completely between waterings.

name: Majesty Palm
notes: Keep soil consistently moist. Loves humidity and regular fertilizing.

name: Nerve Plant
notes: Dramatic — will wilt visibly when thirsty but recovers quickly after watering.

name: Tradescantia
notes: Fast-growing trailing plant. More light = more vibrant purple colour.

name: Succulent Mix
notes: General guideline for most succulents. Drainage holes essential.

name: Lavender
notes: Needs excellent drainage and lots of sun. Allow to dry between waterings.

name: Rosemary
notes: Mediterranean herb — prefers dry conditions. Loves a sunny windowsill.
